name: Tortilla Soup with Cheese
recipeIngredient: 2 Chile ancho, dried, seeded and deveined
8 Ounces Muenster or Monterey Jack, cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the broth flavoring: In a medium-size skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic cloves, and fry until both are deep golden-brown (12 to 15 minutes). Transfer the onion and garlic to a blender, add the tomatoes, and blend until smooth.
2. Prepare the broth: Stir the chicken stock into the tomato mixture. Partially cover and simmer over medium heat for 30 minutes, seasoning with salt.
3. Prepare the garnishing ingredients: If the tortillas are fresh or moist, allow them to dry out in a single layer for a few minutes. Slice the tortillas in half, then slice each half crosswise into 1/4 inch strips. Heat 1/3 cup vegetable oil in a medium-small skillet over medium-high heat. When hot, add the tortilla strips and fry, turning frequently, until crisp. Drain on paper towels. Cut the chiles into 1-inch squares and fry briefly (about 3 to 4 seconds) in the hot oil; remove and drain. Place the fried chiles in a small serving bowl.
4. Assemble the soup: Just before serving, divide the cheese among 4 to 6 bowls. Top with the fried tortilla strips. Ladle the hot soup into the bowls and serve immediately. Pass the toasted chiles and lime alongside.
